talk_title: AACR Progress and the GLITZ Clinical Trial

biomarkers.name: multi-biomarker informed treatment approach
biomarkers.finding: Assessing feasibility to spare gastroesophageal cancer patients from life-altering surgery.

MOA_summary: The speaker introduced the GLITZ clinical trial, which utilizes a multi-biomarker informed treatment approach to determine if patients with gastroesophageal cancer can safely avoid surgery. The trial's IND application has been approved by the FDA and Health Canada.
key_findings: The GLITZ Clinical Trial IND application was approved by the US FDA and Health Canada to assess multi-biomarker informed treatment in gastroesophageal cancer.
Cancer mortality has decreased by nearly 35% since the peak in the 1990s.
The number of cancer survivors in the US has increased by approximately 6.6 million over the last 15 years.
Over 20 new anticancer therapeutics were FDA approved between July 2024 and June 2025.

talk_title: Targeting Programmed Cell Death in Leukemia
MOA_summary: The speaker described the translational journey from basic protein chemistry to the clinical application of targeting programmed cell death pathways. This work culminated in the development and use of venetoclax for the treatment of AML and CLL.
key_findings: Targeting programmed cell death pathways is an effective strategy for treating chronic lymphocytic leukemia and acute myelogenous leukemia.
Venetoclax was successfully translated from laboratory research into a standard of care for AML patients.
